seoTitle: How to Connect GitHub to Power BI for Repo Tracking
metaDescription: Learn how to connect GitHub to Power BI so you can pull repository and commit data into dashboards for tracking development activity and team output.
intro: Engineering teams generate a huge amount of activity data in GitHub, but it rarely gets turned into something leadership can actually look at. This guide walks through connecting GitHub to Power BI so repository and commit history can feed into visual reports. It's built for developers and project managers who want clearer visibility into code contributions without manually exporting spreadsheets.

steps.heading: Set up GitHub access
steps.body: Generate the credentials Power BI needs to read your repository data, typically a personal access token with the appropriate read scopes for your GitHub account or organization.

steps.heading: Connect Power BI to GitHub
steps.body: In Power BI Desktop, use Get Data to start a new connection, enter your repository details, and authenticate with the token or credentials you generated.

steps.heading: Import and shape the data
steps.body: Pull in the repos, commits, and related fields you need through Power Query, filtering and cleaning the data so only the relevant history comes through.

steps.heading: Build visuals around commit activity
steps.body: Create charts and tables that track commits over time, contributor activity, or repository comparisons to surface useful development trends.

steps.heading: Refresh the connection regularly
steps.body: Set up scheduled refreshes so your dashboard keeps reflecting new commits and repository changes without manual re-importing.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to a GitHub repo to connect it to Power BI?
faq.answer: You need at minimum read access to the repository, and a personal access token scoped for that access, though admin rights aren't strictly required for public read data.

faq.question: Can Power BI show data from multiple GitHub repositories at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can import data from several repositories and combine or compare them within the same Power BI report.

faq.question: How often does the GitHub data update in Power BI?
faq.answer: Data only updates when the report is refreshed, so scheduling automatic refreshes keeps commit and repo data reasonably current.

faq.question: Is a paid GitHub plan required for this integration?
faq.answer: Basic repository and commit data is generally accessible through the GitHub API on free accounts, provided you have proper read permissions.
